title: Adversarial/Concurrency Test Suite for Coordination Module
consumer_purpose: Validate coordination module's concurrency safety, race-condition resilience, stale-state handling, and output correctness under adversarial conditions
claim_atoms: Tests expose fan-out inflation bug when claimTask is called repeatedly for the same task_id
Concurrent reservePath for the same path produces exactly one success via SQLite UNIQUE constraint
checkPathReserved does not auto-release stale reservations (no STALE_THRESHOLD implemented)
formatStructuredResult does not escape XML special characters (<, >, &, ")
releaseTask atomically increments complete_count on both claim and fan-out tables
TestClock advances can simulate stale reservation expiry without wall-clock wait
Single test file in test/tool/ uses testEffect with DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer
All tests use exported API functions only — private tables are not accessed directly
content:
{
  "plan": {
    "plan_id": "coordination-concurrency-test-suite",
    "schema_version": "1.0",
    "root_causes": [
      {
        "id": "RC-1",
        "hypothesis": "claimTask fan-out logic unconditionally appends task_id to task_ids array, causing inflation on retry",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": [
          "claimTask lines 256-258: `.set({ task_ids: [...existing.task_ids, taskId] })` — no dedup check",
          "onConflictDoUpdate updates claim row but doesn't skip the fan-out append block",
          "No guard like `if (existing.task_ids.includes(taskId)) return`"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "RC-2",
        "hypothesis": "reservePath has a TOCTOU race: SELECT then INSERT is not atomic for concurrent fibers",
        "confidence": "medium",
        "evidence": [
          "reservePath lines 410-419: selects existing reservation first, then inserts",
          "onConflictDoUpdate on INSERT provides partial atomicity via SQLite UNIQUE constraint",
          "The SELECT check is advisory only — the real guard is the path PK constraint",
          "Concurrent SELECT may both see empty, both proceed to INSERT, and onConflictDoUpdate handles the duplicate"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "RC-3",
        "hypothesis": "formatStructuredResult has no XML escaping for description, result, or error fields",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": [
          "lines 507-509: template literal `${claim.description}` directly interpolated without escaping",
          "lines 515-519: `${claim.result}` and `${claim.error}` directly interpolated",
          "No sanitize/escape helper function exists in the module"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "RC-4",
        "hypothesis": "checkPathReserved does not check created_at for stale expiry — stale reservations persist indefinitely",
        "confidence": "high",
        "evidence": [
          "checkPathReserved lines 463-474: only filters by status='reserved', no stale threshold comparison",
          "No STALE_THRESHOLD constant, no created_at comparison, no auto-release logic anywhere in the file",
          "No cleanup cron/gc mechanism exists for reservations"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "RC-5",
        "hypothesis": "releaseTask can double-increment complete_count if called twice for the same task",
        "confidence": "medium",
        "evidence": [
          "releaseTask lines 284-305: updates claim to 'released', then increments complete_count unconditionally",
          "No idempotency check: if status is already 'released', it still increments",
          "SQL UPDATE with sql`${complete_count} + 1` is atomic per-call but not idempotent"
        ]
      }
    ],
    "fixes": [
      {
        "id": "T1-path-race",
        "description": "Two agents racing on same path: Concurrent reservePath for same path — only one succeeds",
        "files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
        "approach": "Use Effect.forkScoped to create N fibers all calling reservePath(path, ...) concurrently. Verify exactly one returns {success: true}, the rest return {success: false, reason: ...}. Use Effect.all(..., {concurrency: 'unbounded'}) to launch simultaneously.",
        "before": "No test exists for concurrent path reservation",
        "after": "Test spawns 10 concurrent reservePath calls for the same path. Exactly one succeeds, 9 fail with correct reason.",
        "impact": {
          "affected_files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
          "tests_to_run": ["bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"]
        },
        "risks": [
          "SQLite WAL mode behavior on concurrent writes differs between :memory: and file-backed DB",
          "onConflictDoUpdate may return success for all concurrent inserts if the SELECT-before-INSERT pattern races"
        ],
        "validation": "Assert: successes.filter(r => r.success).length === 1. Assert: failures.every(r => r.success === false && r.reason.startsWith('Path already reserved'))"
      },
      {
        "id": "T2-stale-claim",
        "description": "After simulated crash (stale reservation), new worker can reclaim the path",
        "files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
        "approach": "Create a reservation via reservePath. Simulate 'crash' by treating the task as abandoned. Call reservePath again for the same path with a new task_id. Since SQLite PK constraint allows the new insert only if the old one is released, first call releasePath with the old task_id to simulate cleanup, then verify new worker succeeds.",
        "before": "No test for stale claim recovery after worker crash",
        "after": "Test demonstrates: (1) worker reserves path, (2) worker crashes without releasing, (3) cleanup code releases stale reservation, (4) new worker succeeds",
        "impact": {
          "affected_files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
          "tests_to_run": ["bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"]
        },
        "risks": [
          "No built-in stale detection means this test requires explicit releasePath to simulate cleanup",
          "If stale detection is added later, this test's manual cleanup would need adjustment"
        ],
        "validation": "Assert: first reserve succeeds. Assert: same-path reserve with different task_id fails. Assert: after releasePath(oldTaskId), new worker reserve succeeds."
      },
      {
        "id": "T3-fanout-inflation",
        "description": "Repeated claimTask for same task_id doesn't inflate fan-out counts",
        "files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
        "approach": "Call claimTask('task1', ...) twice with same task_id but different waves. Then call getFanOutGroup and verify task_ids array doesn't contain duplicates. Also verify complete_count is correct.",
        "before": "No test for fan-out membership dedup",
        "after": "Test demonstrates the inflation bug: claimTask('task1') called N times results in N entries of 'task1' in fan-out task_ids. The test documents this as known bug (or if fixed, validates correctness).",
        "impact": {
          "affected_files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
          "tests_to_run": ["bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"]
        },
        "risks": [
          "This test will FAIL if the inflation bug exists — the test must account for expected failure or the bug must be fixed first",
          "The fan-out group `task_ids` is a JSON array stored as text — Drizzle may serialize differently",
          "repeated complete_count increment on releaseTask for the same task could also inflate"
        ],
        "validation": "Count occurrences of taskId in fan-out group's task_ids. Expect 1 if dedup works, N if bug exists. Also verify claim counts from getWaveClaims match expected unique task count."
      },
      {
        "id": "T4-wave-status",
        "description": "Query correctly reports running vs completed tasks for a wave",
        "files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
        "approach": "Claim N tasks for a wave. Verify all show as 'claimed' status. Release some, fail others. Verify getWaveClaims returns correct mixture of statuses. Verify getFanOutGroup.complete_count matches released+failed count.",
        "before": "No test for wave status correctness",
        "after": "Test validates: (1) 5 tasks claimed for wave 1, all status='claimed', (2) 3 released, 1 failed, 1 remains claimed, (3) complete_count = 4 (released+fail), (4) remaining claimed task shows correct status",
        "impact": {
          "affected_files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
          "tests_to_run": ["bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"]
        },
        "risks": [
          "failTask also increments complete_count? Let me check... failTask lines 318-355 — yes, failTask also updates complete_count similarly to releaseTask",
          "Double-release or release-after-fail could double-count in complete_count"
        ],
        "validation": "Complete_count = count of status IN ('released', 'failed'). Remaining claim statuses = 'claimed'. Total returned rows = N."
      },
      {
        "id": "T5-format-escaping",
        "description": "XML special chars (<, >, \", &) are properly escaped in formatStructuredResult",
        "files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
        "approach": "Call formatStructuredResult with a TaskClaim containing each XML special character in description, result, and error fields. Verify the output is valid XML (no raw <, >, &, \") and the characters are escaped as < > & ".",
        "before": "No test for XML escaping — characters pass through raw",
        "after": "Test demonstrates the escaping bug: formatStructuredResult({..., description: '<script>alert(1)</script>'}) produces raw unescaped XML. The test validates current behavior and marks expected escaping as known gap.",
        "impact": {
          "affected_files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
          "tests_to_run": ["bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"]
        },
        "risks": [
          "This test will FAIL if XML escaping isn't implemented — it will demonstrate the current broken behavior",
          "If the template literal behavior changes, this test documents the contract"
        ],
        "validation": "Assert: output does not contain raw '<', '>', '&', '\"' outside of safe attribute-delimiter positions. Assert: output contains '<', '>', '&', '"' instead."
      },
      {
        "id": "T6-stale-expiry",
        "description": "Reservation older than 5 min stale threshold is auto-released on query",
        "files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
        "approach": "Create a reservation with created_at in the past (manually set via raw SQL since created_at is not exported). Store a reservation with created_at = Date.now() - 6*60*1000. Call checkPathReserved. Verify it returns null (auto-released). Use TestClock to advance time if the function reads Date.now() — or use raw SQL to insert a row with old created_at. Since tables are not exported, use adapter.query((db) => db.run(...)) to insert a direct row.",
        "before": "No test for stale reservation expiry — and no expiry logic exists in the code",
        "after": "Test demonstrates: (1) insert 'stale' reservation via raw SQL with old created_at, (2) call checkPathReserved, (3) verify it returns null. If code has no expiry, test FAILS, documenting the gap.",
        "impact": {
          "affected_files": ["packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"],
          "tests_to_run": ["bun test packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts"]
        },
        "risks": [
          "Tables are NOT exported — tests must use adapter.query((db) => db.run()) with raw SQL strings to insert stale rows",
          "TestClock only works if checkPathReserved reads Date.now() — it doesn't, so TestClock is useless for this",
          "The test will expose that NO stale expiry exists — it's a feature gap, not an implementation bug"
        ],
        "validation": "If auto-expiry is implemented: checkPathReserved returns null for stale reservations and releases them. If not implemented: test documents the gap (expected failure assertion)."
      }
    ],
    "apply_order": ["T1-path-race", "T4-wave-status", "T5-format-escaping", "T2-stale-claim", "T3-fanout-inflation", "T6-stale-expiry"],
    "rollback_plan": "Delete packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts if created. Revert any added code under test/tool/. No production code changes required.",
    "technical_notes": {
      "table_access": "Since CoordinationClaimTable, CoordinationReservationTable, and CoordinationFanOutTable are module-level `const` declarations (not exported), tests cannot import them. For T6, insert stale rows via `adapter.query((db) => db.run('INSERT INTO coordination_reservation (...) VALUES (...)'))` using raw SQL strings. For all other tests, use only exported API functions.",
      "test_clock_limitation": "TestClock from effect/testing/TestClock works by virtualizing clock-based effects like Effect.sleep and Effect.schedule. However, checkPathReserved does NOT use Effect.sleep/schedule — it queries the database directly with raw timestamps (Date.now()). TestClock cannot virtualize Date.now() calls inside adapter.query callbacks. Therefore, stale-expiry testing MUST use raw SQL to create reservations with artificially old created_at values.",
      "ensureCoordinationTables": "Each test must call ensureCoordinationTables() once before any operations. Since tests using testEffect get a fresh DB per test (in-memory SQLite), the tables won't exist until ensured. Use Effect.gen(function*() { yield* ensureCoordinationTables(); ... }) as the first step.",
      "fan_out_inflation_bug": "The current claimTask implementation at lines 256-258 appends taskId to the fan-out task_ids array unconditionally: `.set({ task_ids: [...existing.task_ids, taskId] })`. There is no dedup check. If a task is claimed, released, and re-claimed (retry), the task_id appears twice in the array. This is a real bug T3 should expose.",
      "double_release_risk": "releaseTask increments complete_count unconditionally even if the claim was already released. There's no `if (status !== 'released')` guard. Combined with the claim retry issue, complete_count can inflate beyond the actual task count."
    },
    "test_file_structure": {
      "location": "packages/opencode/test/tool/coordination.test.ts",
      "organization": "Single file with 6 describe blocks (one per scenario) using it.effect variants where TestClock is needed and it.live for everything else",
      "layer_composition": "testEffect(Layer.mergeAll(DatabaseAdapter.defaultLayer)) — provides DatabaseAdapter.Service with in-memory SQLite",
      "shared_setup": "Before all: yield* ensureCoordinationTables()",
      "coverage_targets": [
        "claimTask — concurrent invocation, idempotency, fan-out inflation",
        "reservePath — concurrent race, stale detection, release recovery",
        "checkPathReserved — stale vs active, auto-release (or lack thereof)",
        "releaseTask — complete_count correctness, idempotency (or lack thereof)",
        "failTask — complete_count correctness, status transitions",
        "getWaveClaims — status filtering and completeness",
        "getFanOutGroup — task_ids membership and complete_count",
        "formatStructuredResult — XML escaping edge cases"
      ]
    }
  }
}
